PhoneActiveSession.description: One active phone allocation for the dashboard overview: a phone currently claimed by a run, a workflow-less interactive lease, or a dedicated phone in use.

allocated_at.description: When the session claimed the phone.

allocated_by.description: AllocatedBy identifies what triggered the allocation (WORKFLOW_EDITOR, DASHBOARD_RUN, API_RUN, INTERACTIVE).

is_dedicated_phone.description: True when the phone is a dedicated/rented phone.

location.description: Rack/data-center the phone is pinned to.

name.description: Optional user-chosen session label; unique among the org's active sessions.

nickname.description: Org's nickname for the phone, if set.

session_id.description: Phone_sessions row id (the canonical session id).

tags.description: Optional key->value labels attached to the session.

workflow_id.description: Workflow tied to the session, nil for an interactive lease.

workflow_name.description: Tied workflow's name, nil for an interactive lease.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

sessions.description: List of active allocations, newest first.

PhoneAllocateRequest.description: Request body for allocating a phone.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

live_view.description: Hosted live-view options for this session; omit for the defaults (token auth, interactive, enabled).

name.description: Optional session label (letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, underscores; max 64). Unique among the org's active sessions - allocating with a name already in use returns a conflict.

phone_id.description: PhoneID pins allocation to a specific device (for dedicated devices).

recording.description: Record this session's screen (default true). false suppresses the video recording and rolling thumbnail entirely - no screen content is ever written.

tags.description: Optional key->value labels for organizing sessions (max 50 tags; keys up to 40 chars, values up to 128).

telemetry.description: Persist this session's telemetry spans (default true). false skips the durable trace store; the live telemetry stream still works while the session runs.

ttl.description: Idle-timeout override for this session; omit for the defaults (inactive after 5 min, close 10 min later).

workflow_id.description: Workflow requesting allocation; nil for an interactive lease.

PhoneAllocateResponse.description: Returned when a device has been successfully allocated.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

control_url.description: WebSocket URL for driving the phone over the device control protocol.

live_view_url.description: Hosted live-view page URL (live screen + interaction, iframe-embeddable). Contains a session-scoped capability token; treat as a secret. Dies with the session.

region.description: Physical region or location of the device.

session_id.description: Device_allocations row id — the canonical session identifier (WS-ID2).

telemetry_url.description: WebSocket URL for the session's live telemetry stream (trace spans + output logs, read-only). Dies with the session.

PhoneAppSummary.description: An app available on fleet phones, with its vision-model support status.

ios_app_store_id.description: App Store identifier for the iOS app.

is_supported.description: Whether automation is supported for this app.

model_datasets.description: Vision datasets available for this app.

model_status.description: Training status of the app's vision model.

model_trained_at.description: When the app's vision model was last trained.

slug.description: URL-safe short name for the app.

updated_at.description: When the app record was last updated.

PhoneAvailableListResponse.description: Returned when querying available devices for allocation.

PhoneDeallocateResponse.description: Returned after a device has been successfully deallocated.

deallocated_at.description: Timestamp when the device was released.

PhoneLiveViewOptions.description: Per-session options for the hosted live view.

auth.description: Live-view auth mode: "token" (default) — capability URL, no sign-in; "org" — tokenless URL requiring sign-in + membership of the session's organization.

disabled.description: Disable the live view for this session: no token is minted and live_view_url is omitted.

view_only.description: Make the live view watch-only (server-enforced; input never reaches the phone).

status.description: 'ready' when a preview URL is returned, or 'pending' when no preview exists.

url.description: URL for the current preview JPEG, set only when status is 'ready'. Time-limited; poll for a fresh image.

monthly_cents.description: Monthly-equivalent spend for this interval's rentals.

PhoneRentalSubscriptionListResponse.description: An org's device rental subscriptions, plus an org-wide summary. The summary counts only active rentals regardless of which statuses the subscriptions list contains.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

summary.description: Org-wide rollup (active count, combined monthly spend, per-interval breakdown, upcoming charges), independent of the page facets.

total.description: Count of rentals matching the request (for pagination; has_more is derivable as offset+len(subscriptions) < total).

cancel_at_period_end.description: Whether the subscription cancels at period end.

canceled_at.description: Timestamp when the subscription was canceled.

current_period_end.description: End of the current billing period.

current_period_start.description: Start of the current billing period.

payment_status.description: Billing health ('healthy' or 'past_due'). Orthogonal to status: a past_due rental stays active while payment is retried.

phone_id.description: Rented phone's public identifier (matches PhoneSummary.phone_id), if a phone is assigned.

phone_name.description: Display name of the rented device.

phone_nickname.description: Organization-assigned nickname of the rented device.

plan_price_cents.description: Plan price in cents per interval.

PhoneRentalSummary.description: Org-wide rollup shown above the rentals table.

active_count.description: Number of active rentals for the org.

combined_monthly_cents.description: Total monthly-equivalent spend across all active rentals (daily/weekly prices normalised to a monthly figure).

per_interval.description: Count + monthly-equivalent spend grouped by plan interval.

upcoming_charges.description: Next 30 days of renewals bucketed by day.

PhoneRentalUpcomingCharge.description: One day's bucket of upcoming rental renewals.

amount_cents.description: Summed unit price of the renewals on this day.

count.description: Number of rentals renewing on this day.

date.description: UTC day the renewals fall on; the client formats it locally.

source.description: 'workflow' when tied to a workflow, else 'interactive'.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

get.description: Lists the API keys for the caller's organization, with optional paging (limit + offset). Ordered newest-first.

post.description: Mints a fresh API key for the caller's organization. The plaintext key value is returned exactly once and never stored or returned again.

delete.description: Revokes an API key. Subsequent requests using its value are rejected as unauthorized.

post.description: Rotates the plaintext value for an existing API key, preserving its name and identifier. The previous value is invalidated immediately.

get.description: Returns the organization's automatic balance top-up configuration and status.
Returns the organization's current credit balance in microdollars plus a display string.
Paginated invoice history for the caller's organization with optional filters and sort.

parameters.description: RFC3339 upper bound for invoice_date (with date_from = an exact calendar month)
parameters.name: date_to

schema.description: RFC3339 upper bound for invoice_date (with date_from = an exact calendar month)

parameters.description: filter to invoices for a dedicated phone
parameters.name: phone_id

schema.description: filter to invoices for a dedicated phone

get.description: Returns every phone rental subscription owned by the caller's organization, in any lifecycle state (active or canceled) - canceled rentals are included so past rentals stay visible. Each item's status field tells them apart. The summary block (active_count, combined monthly cost) counts only active rentals.
Returns the caller organization's active subscription plan. Only an active subscription is returned; if the org has none, this responds 404. (The status field is therefore always 'active' here.)

post.description: Allocates a phone and opens a session. Omit workflow_id for an interactive lease (drive the phone directly); set it to allocate for a workflow. Pass phone_id to pin a specific dedicated phone. If allocation setup fails the claim is rolled back, so you are never billed for a session that never starts.

get.description: Returns the apps the platform supports orchestration for, optionally filtered by platform and category.

Returns the phones the caller can start a session on right now: every active phone in the shared pool, plus the caller org's own dedicated phones that are currently free. Only free + active phones appear here, so a dedicated phone that is busy or offline is intentionally absent - use GET /phones/my to see the org's full dedicated inventory including in-use ones. Optionally filtered by phone_type; counts by type are included alongside the list.

parameters.description: only return phones of this type
parameters.name: phone_type

schema.description: only return phones of this type

get.summary: List phones ready to start a session

post.description: Deallocates a phone the caller's org currently holds. The session is billed and the phone is torn down asynchronously.

get.description: Returns the caller org's full dedicated (private/rented) phone inventory - every state, not just the free ones: busy phones in an active session, offline/inactive phones, and phones in maintenance are all included, so this is the endpoint to discover a phone_id you can pin via POST /phones/allocate. Each phone's current_session_id and status reflect its live state. include_expired=true also keeps rentals past their rental_expires_at so users can see what they used to own. Filter by status/phone_type and paginate; the response total is the full match count.

parameters.description: include rented devices whose rental window has expired
parameters.name: include_expired

schema.description: include rented devices whose rental window has expired

parameters.description: free-text search across nickname, name, model, location
parameters.name: search

schema.description: free-text search across nickname, name, model, location

parameters.description: only phones whose rental expires at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: rental_expires_after

schema.description: only phones whose rental expires at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only phones whose rental expires at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: rental_expires_before

schema.description: only phones whose rental expires at/before this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only phones last seen at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_active_after

schema.description: only phones last seen at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only phones last seen at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_active_before

schema.description: only phones last seen at/before this RFC3339 time

get.description: Returns one page of the org's phone sessions for the Session Inspector table: active/unbilled sessions pinned on top, terminal history paginated beneath. Covers workflow runs and workflow-less interactive leases; each row links to a session. Filters: search, workflow_id, status.

parameters.description: filter by source: workflow and/or interactive; repeatable
parameters.name: source

schema.description: filter by source: workflow and/or interactive; repeatable

parameters.description: filter by type: shared and/or dedicated; repeatable
parameters.name: dedicated

schema.description: filter by type: shared and/or dedicated; repeatable

parameters.description: only sessions started at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: started_after

schema.description: only sessions started at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only sessions started at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: started_before

schema.description: only sessions started at/before this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only sessions de-allocated at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: ended_after

schema.description: only sessions de-allocated at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only sessions de-allocated at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: ended_before

schema.description: only sessions de-allocated at/before this RFC3339 time

get.description: Returns one page of the organization's currently-active phone sessions joined with phone + workflow display fields: in-flight runs, workflow-less interactive leases, and dedicated phones in use. Paginated via limit (default 25, max 100) + offset; the response total is the full active count.

parameters.description: case-insensitive match on phone name/nickname/id, session id, or workflow name
parameters.name: search

schema.description: case-insensitive match on phone name/nickname/id, session id, or workflow name

get.description: Returns one session for the Session Inspector: session lifecycle + phone display fields + workflow name (when tied to one) + an inlined presigned recording URL. Works for active and terminal sessions, and for workflow runs and workflow-less interactive leases. Org-scoped: another org's session reads as not found.

Returns a short-lived URL for the session's screen recording, keyed on session_id — so it works for workflow runs and workflow-less interactive leases alike. Status is "pending" (no URL) when the recording hasn't finished uploading yet. Org-scoped: another org's session reads as not found.

Returns a short-lived URL for the session's current screen thumbnail — a rolling JPEG refreshed every few seconds while the session is active. Poll this endpoint and swap the image; every call mints a fresh URL. Status is "pending" (no URL) before the first frame lands or after the session ends. Org-scoped: another org's session reads as not found.
Returns a single phone by its identifier.

patch.description: Sets the human-readable display name on a private phone the caller's org owns. Returns the updated phone summary.

get.description: Returns a short-lived URL for the phone's current screen preview — a rolling JPEG refreshed every few seconds while the phone is paired, available with or without an active session. Poll this endpoint and swap the image; every call mints a fresh URL. Status is "pending" when no preview exists yet. Authorized to the org that owns the phone or currently holds its active session; any other org reads as not found.
get.summary: Get a phone's live screen preview

post.description: Requests an on-demand factory reset of a private phone the caller's org owns. Requires the phone to be ACTIVE and not currently allocated. Sets the phone to MAINTENANCE while the wipe is carried out.

Returns paginated recent (non-archived) runs the caller started - scoped to their own user within the org, not every member's runs. Filters: workflow_id, search (run ID substring), status, trigger. Sortable fields: status, started_at, completed_at, created_at, workflow_id.
Returns paginated run events for a session, filtered by session_id.
Returns paginated historic runs for the caller's user. Use POST /runs for recent (non-archived) runs.

get.description: Returns total run count + success rate for the given workflow, scoped to the caller's user.
Returns one run by ID, scoped to the caller's organization.

patch.description: Cancels a run that is still queued or running, scoped to the caller's org. A run that has already reached a terminal state (completed/failed/cancelled) cannot be cancelled and reads as not found.

post.description: Creates one or more runs against the given workflow and queues them for execution. Pre-flight checks: balance sufficient, concurrency limit, workflow exists. Runs that fail to queue are marked FAILED immediately so they stop counting toward the concurrency limit.
Paginated, filterable list of inference calls (detect + locate) the caller's user was billed for. Filters: date range, endpoint, free-text search. Ordered by call time DESC.

get.description: Returns infrastructure cost and compute-minute summaries for the caller's user over a date range, plus per-bucket chart data. Granularity is hourly (≤24h window) or daily. Pass the window and granularity as query params.

parameters.description: IANA timezone for bucketing periods (e.g., America/Los_Angeles)
parameters.name: timezone

schema.description: IANA timezone for bucketing periods (e.g., America/Los_Angeles)

get.description: Paginated list of workflows in the caller's org, with optional search, status, platform, and created/last-run date filters via query params.

parameters.description: free-text search across workflow name or ID substring
parameters.name: search

schema.description: free-text search across workflow name or ID substring

parameters.description: only workflows created at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: created_after

schema.description: only workflows created at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only workflows created at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: created_before

schema.description: only workflows created at/before this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only workflows last run at/after this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_run_after

schema.description: only workflows last run at/after this RFC3339 time

parameters.description: only workflows last run at/before this RFC3339 time
parameters.name: last_run_before

schema.description: only workflows last run at/before this RFC3339 time

post.description: Creates a workflow in the caller's org. Name must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$ and be unique within the org. Pass code to save the workflow's first code revision atomically with it; omit it to create an empty workflow and add code later. Returns the workflow_id (plus revision_id and revision when code was provided).

delete.description: Deletes a workflow. Org-scoped — workflows in other orgs return 404.

get.description: Returns a single workflow, scoped to the caller's org (workflows in other orgs return 404).

put.description: Applies a partial update (name, platform, status, ocr_engine). Org-scoped — workflows in other orgs return 404.

get.description: Returns the source code of the workflow's current revision, scoped to the caller's org.

post.description: Persists a new revision of the workflow's code. Hash-deduplicates against the current revision (no-op if unchanged). Source is capped at 256KB.
Creates a new revision with the source of the named revision. Does NOT dedup against the current revision so the action is auditable in the revision history.

get.description: Returns revision metadata (id, number, author, message, bytes, sha256, created_at) for the workflow, in reverse-chronological order. Use the `before` cursor to paginate older revisions.

parameters.description: cursor: return revisions older than this revision number
parameters.name: before

schema.description: cursor: return revisions older than this revision number

get.description: Returns a single revision including its full source. Defense-in-depth: the revision must belong to the named workflow or it's treated as missing.
